title: 403 Error when saving SAML SSO configuration
symptom: When attempting to save the SAML SSO configuration in n8n, a 403 error is returned, preventing the configuration from being saved.
not_this_if: Error occurs in a cloud-hosted n8n instance
root_cause: The 403 error indicates that the request is forbidden, which is often due to misconfigured OAuth client ID/secret or unauthorized domains in the Google Cloud Console. If the n8n instance address is not included in the authorized JavaScript origins or redirect URIs, the request will fail.
fix_steps: Go to the Google Cloud Console and select your project.
Navigate to the 'Credentials' section and find your OAuth 2.0 Client IDs.
Check the 'Authorized JavaScript origins' and 'Authorized redirect URIs' to ensure they include the n8n instance address.
Update the settings if necessary and save the changes.
Try saving the SAML SSO configuration in n8n again.
agent_instruction: Verify and update the OAuth client ID/secret configuration in the Google Cloud Console.
